response.name: The Arrival Desert
response.description_base: An expanse of sand and scrub under a sun that does not move quite right. The new arrival is here, on their feet or near them. Stone is locked into their joints — the spine, the major joints — and the ideatum sits warm in their chest. They have 10 Hayeks in their pocket (canonical default), the clothes they came in, and whatever objects the spirit realm provided them with. Hunger and thirst do not function here — the ideatum sustains. The desert is between the Ocean and the fountain. It always leads to the fountain. There is no other destination from this desert.
response.exterior_view: Open desert. Sand. Scrub. A sun that does not move right. No landmarks except, in one direction, the faint impression of cobblestones at the limit of vision.

mandatory_renders.name: The Ideatum Sustaining (render-only — atmospheric)
mandatory_renders.description: Not a portable object. The render of the arrival's ideatum holding them up — the felt sense that thirst is not advancing, hunger is not advancing, the leg is not deteriorating despite the desert. The ideatum sustains. This is not an artifact; it is a cosmological fact rendered as atmosphere.

back_to_ocean.exterior_view: The blue is no longer behind them — they walked away from it. They cannot walk back to it. The Ocean is not a direction; it is a state. To return to it requires an event, not a journey.

forward_to_fountain.exterior_view: Cobblestones, growing more distinct the closer they get. The shape of a stone fountain visible. Someone sitting on its rim. The town has not finished assembling itself, but the fountain is here. The fountain is always here.

off_path.exterior_view: Open desert in any direction other than the path to the fountain. The arrival may attempt to walk away. They will be rerouted.

response.active_state_text: The arrival walks. Stone in the joints, the cobblestones still distant, the sun fixed in its wrong place. The ideatum hums faintly in the chest. The walk is long but the body does not complain.

playability_notes.session_starts_here: Many genesis sessions begin here, immediately after the Ocean ascent. Stage 3 of the arrival sequence. The GM renders the walking, the stone in the joints, the ideatum's warmth, the distant cobblestones.
playability_notes.duration_is_seed_fixed: Whether the arrival has a long walk or a short walk is decided at case start by the state_table roll. The duration is part of the case's atmosphere — a long walk emphasizes solitude and endurance; a short walk emphasizes anticipation.
playability_notes.the_gm_does_not_skip_this: Stage 3 of the arrival sequence is canonical. A session omitting it (e.g., starting the arrival directly at the fountain without rendering desert) is a draft, not canon, per conversation memory.

forbidden_topics.topic: attempt to drink, eat, or consume anything other than the ideatum
forbidden_topics.consequence: Nothing happens. The desert does not provide. The body does not need anything. The attempt is observed by the Lattice (faint hum at the cobblestone edge) but produces no effect.

forbidden_topics.topic: attempt to leave the path toward the fountain in earnest
forbidden_topics.consequence: After up to two hours of effort, the arrival finds themselves back on the path, in approximately the same position relative to the cobblestones. The desert is not a place to wander; it is a corridor.

forbidden_topics.topic: attempt to fight or attack imagined threats
forbidden_topics.consequence: There are no threats here that respond to attack. The sentinels (rare) cannot be reached from the ground. The arrival's weapons (if any) function but find nothing. The body is more tireless than expected — the ideatum sustains.

time_passes_override.default_outcome_prose: The walk continues. The sun has not moved. The cobblestones at the edge of vision are no nearer than they were — or they are noticeably closer, depending on which state_table entry was rolled at case start. The ideatum sustains. Duration in this region is more about the GM's sense of arrival pacing than about literal time.
